non_blocking_observations: within-position baseline Pearson/Spearman of -1 is the documented LOOCV group-mean artifact

shared-source dependency: the candidate artifact supplies both input features (2022-2024) and the outcome layer (2025); disjoint windows, not leakage, but a systematic upstream error would touch both sides -- mitigated by the section-4 promotion path
follow_up_items: 2025 outcomes include partial-season players (legitimate but high-variance targets)

assessment: 41.9% joined-population MAE reduction vs baseline; rank correlation moved from -0.16 to 0.75
shuffled control landed at/below baseline in every view: the sanity arm worked, attributing the improvement to actual history values rather than model structure
every position improved 34-40% while its shuffled arm sat at baseline; no position carried or degraded the result
no-history subgroup shows the expected null: real ~= shuffled, both slightly better than raw position mean for structural (imputation-centering) reasons only
explicitly a candidate signal, not production evidence
limits: candidate (not promoted) source for both inputs and outcomes
one fold design, one lambda, one shuffle seed, one baseline definition
attribution unknown within the 26-column set; prior-year PPR plausibly carries much of the lift (ablation required)
partial-season outcome rows leave an outlier/leverage question open
single season pair; no cross-season stability evidence

q3_required_robustness_checks_prioritize.reason: attribution is the central open question; reuses the toggleable #104 families
stronger simple comparator; calibrates the honest size of the marginal gain
confirms the result is not an artifact of one regularization choice
bounds the variance of the null arm
partial-season outcomes are legitimate but high-variance targets

q4_upstream_tiber_data.stance: production binding must not occur from a candidate/not-promoted source; any binding path requires TIBER-Data to promote or replace player_season_coverage_v0 with a governed promoted artifact (own gates: schema, provenance allow-list, reproducibility, coverage acceptance, owner review); Forecast continues treating the current mirrors as experiment inputs only

issue_sketch.title: TIBER-Data: promote player_season_coverage_v0 after source-backed governance review
issue_sketch.scope: define promotion criteria (schema, provenance allow-list, reproducibility from the committed builder, per-season/position coverage acceptance, non-goals), run the review, and either promote with a promotion record or state precisely what blocks promotion

q5_production_binding_prerequisites_not_: section-3 robustness checks completed and reviewed with the signal surviving ablation and the stronger baseline
structural leakage gate in the production path
sha-pinned allow-listed source/provenance gate in the production path
formalized train-fold null-handling policy with documented fully-null fallback
monitoring and regression tests (golden values, drift checks)
defined reviewed fallback behavior for no-history players
strict separation from product advice/rankings surfaces
owner review of the binding proposal itself before any seasonalPprModel.ts or route change

q6_recommended_next_issue.title: Forecast: run robustness checks for candidate player-history signal
q6_recommended_next_issue.scope: implement the P1-P5 checks inside the existing isolated experiment path (same fail-closed preflight, same experimental marking, same decision-enum ceiling), producing one durable comparison report
q6_recommended_next_issue.rationale: robustness is cheaper than promotion, stays within Forecast, and its outcome determines whether upstream promotion is worth requesting at all; the TIBER-Data promotion issue follows only if the signal survives

non_goals_confirmed: no new model run computed for this review
